dialogueChunks.intro: Forgive me, {player_name}. I'm not usually like this. It's just... the house is so quiet now.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: My husband passed three weeks ago. Not violently, not from {enemy_type}: just a fever that wouldn't break. He was the one who managed our stores, kept the pantry full, made sure {location} always had enough to eat. Now the shelves are empty because I don't know how to do what he did. {target_npc} has been helping, but they have their own family to feed. I need {quest_item} from the suppliers he used to visit {location_hint}, but I don't know the routes and the roads are dangerous.
dialogueChunks.outro: I'm not asking for pity. I'm asking for help until I can stand on my own two feet again.

playerResponses.text: You don't need to stand alone yet. I'll get what you need.
Grief is hard enough without empty shelves. Tell me the route.
I'm sorry for your loss, but I can't take on every sad story.

dialogueChunks.intro: {player_name}, do you see that garden over there? The one with the withered tomatoes? That was my daughter's.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: She was taken by {enemy_type} during a raid on the outskirts of {location} last month. Not captured: taken. {target_npc} tried to reach her but there were too many. Now the garden is dying because I can't bring myself to tend it: every time I touch the soil, I feel her hands guiding mine. But she'd hate to see it like this. She'd want it to thrive. I need {quest_item} to restore it properly: good seed, fresh soil, the things she always said made the difference. The supplier is {location_hint}, and the roads aren't safe.
dialogueChunks.outro: I know a garden won't bring her back. But letting it die feels like losing her twice.

playerResponses.text: A garden kept alive is a memory kept alive. I'll get what you need.
She sounds like she was special. I'll honor her work.
I'm sorry. But a garden isn't something I risk my life for.

dialogueChunks.intro: I should be cooking right now, {player_name}. That's what I do: I cook. But I can't seem to remember how anything tastes.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: My brother was my taster, my critic, my loudest supporter. Every recipe I ever created, he was the first to try it and the last to leave the table. He died on the road from {location} two months ago, ambushed by {enemy_type} while delivering {quest_item} to a neighboring settlement. The delivery never arrived, and neither did he. {target_npc} found his pack scattered {location_hint}, but the supplies were gone. I want to finish what he started: complete that delivery in his name. But I need to gather replacement {quest_item} first.
dialogueChunks.outro: He believed every meal could make someone's day better. The least I can do is make sure his last delivery reaches the people who needed it.

playerResponses.text: Completing an unfinished delivery. That's a worthy memorial.
I'm sorry about your brother. But retracing his steps feels dangerous.
